target.name: Databricks
target.founder: Ali Ghodsi, Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia, Patrick Wendell, Reynold Xin, Andy Konwinski, Arsalan Tavakoli-Shiraji

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Databricks, a Series L AI & Software company, is valued at $134B following $24.7B in total capital raised. The valuation benchmark draws from a comparable set of 8 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier peers spanning both private (7) and public (1) stages, including Palantir, OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Waymo. This composition reflects the frontier-technology concentration in the peer universe.

Peer-median valuation methodology was applied as the primary triangulation approach. The median across the 8-company cohort stands at $154.5B, with a range spanning $29.3B (floor) to $400.0B (ceiling). This method directly anchors the valuation analysis given comparable company positioning and sector dynamics. No additional methods (DCF, precedent transaction, or revenue-multiple approaches) were determinable from available data.

The triangulated valuation range centers on $154.5B median, with sensitivity bounds of $131.3B (low) and $177.7B (high), implying a 15.3% downside and 32.5% upside relative to the current $134B mark. Databricks' position near the lower quartile of the peer set reflects its Series L stage relative to public comparables; however, material uncertainty persists around private peer valuations, liquidity timing, and denominator risk concentration in frontier AI. The single-method triangulation constrains confidence; validation via revenue multiples or ARR-based approaches would strengthen conviction.
